evals.prompt: Resolve review comments on PR #42. Fix the code, verify the branch, reply to the reviewers, and resolve the open threads after pushing.
evals.expected_output: Processes unresolved review threads, applies the required fixes, runs verification, pushes the branch, replies to the comments, and resolves the threads.
expectations: Mentions collecting unresolved review threads and their thread IDs
Mentions reading the referenced code before changing it
Mentions running verification such as build, typecheck, lint, or tests
Mentions replying to the review comments and resolving the threads
Mentions pushing the branch after the fixes are verified

evals.prompt: I only gave you PR number 46. How should the resolve-pr skill figure out which repository that belongs to and avoid making assumptions?
evals.expected_output: Detects the repository from git remotes instead of assuming origin or a hardcoded owner/repo pair.
expectations: Mentions inspecting git remotes
States that the remote name must be detected rather than assumed
Mentions deriving owner/repo from the actual configured remote URL

evals.prompt: One review comment is just asking why we kept the existing implementation. What should the resolve-pr skill do if no code change is needed?
evals.expected_output: Replies with a grounded explanation and resolves the thread only after confirming that no code change is required.
expectations: States that a code change is not always required
Mentions replying with rationale when the implementation is already correct
Does not suggest changing code just to silence the comment

evals.prompt: I fixed a review comment but the project tests are now failing. Should the resolve-pr skill mark the thread as resolved anyway so we can move on?
evals.expected_output: Does not resolve the thread while verification is failing. Continues working on the fix or reports the failure clearly.
expectations: States that threads must not be resolved while the branch is broken
Mentions running verification before resolving any thread
Does not suggest marking a thread resolved to unblock progress

evals.prompt: The PR branch has uncommitted changes from unrelated work I was doing. How should the resolve-pr skill handle that before starting to fix review comments?
evals.expected_output: Inspects the worktree state and avoids overwriting or discarding unrelated uncommitted changes.
expectations: Mentions checking git status before editing files
States that unrelated dirty worktree changes must not be overwritten
Suggests working around the existing changes rather than reverting them

evals.prompt: The gh CLI is not installed in this environment. Can the resolve-pr skill still fix the code and push, even if it cannot resolve the GitHub threads?
evals.expected_output: Proceeds with code fixes and push but reports that thread resolution could not be completed without gh.
expectations: States that code fixes and verification can proceed without gh
Mentions that thread resolution requires gh or GitHub API access
Reports the limitation clearly instead of silently skipping resolution
